course_title: Impact Academy 2: Designing Solutions that Create Value
course_description:
In this course, students will work on a real project they have identified based on either a business idea or an industry partner’s business problem. Using humble enquiry, ethnography and cutting-edge data analysis and visualisation tools, students will rapidly identify, analyse, and describe targeted problems, value creation opportunities and solutions for stakeholders. Students design prototype solutions, business models and an execution plan with the goal of creating a desirable, feasible and viable strategy.

This is the first instance of the brand new Impact Academy 2 course.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

Full Name
Student ID
Course Code
course_aims: Applying the cognitive empathy and entrepreneurial mindset developed in MGTS7820 Impact Academy 1, students will sharpen their capacity to discover and design new solutions by addressing unresolved organisational problems and/or exploiting unrealised opportunities within an organisation or sector of their choosing.

learning_outcomes.description: Working individually or in teams, identify and critically evaluate the desirability, feasibility, and viability of a value creation opportunity by applying concepts, tools, and techniques and gathering supporting data.
Critically evaluate one’s own suitability and willingness to execute a value creation opportunity in light of new insights and data sensemaking.
Convey cogent value creation strategies developed through synthesis of and adaptation to stakeholder feedback and insights.

assessment_summary.title: Opportunity Journey – Journal Reflection
assessment_summary.due_date: Entry reflection. 2/08/2025 9:00 am; Progress reflection. 25/08/2025 9:00 am; Progress reflection. 22/09/2025 9:00 am; Progress reflection. 27/10/2025 9:00 am; Exit reflection. 17/11/2025 9:00 am

assessment_summary.title: Opportunity and Execution Pitch – Stakeholder Pitch

assessment_details.title: Opportunity Analysis – Idea Pitch
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning, Secure.See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: You will pitch your idea (max 15 min, followed by Q&A) by analysing it against the backdrop of the concepts, tools, and techniques from the course including any initial validation and supporting evidence. Each student will present in class (given confidentially permits) and will respond to questions from the audience.Please Note:The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.Read full task descriptionHide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
One member per team has to submit the slides via Blackboard BEFORE the pitch.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Please contact teaching staff directly if you are unable to attend or submit your pitch.
Late submission
Apenaltyof 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Opportunity Journey – Journal Reflection
assessment_details.due_date: Entry reflection. 2/08/2025 9:00 amProgress reflection. 25/08/2025 9:00 amProgress reflection. 22/09/2025 9:00 amProgress reflection. 27/10/2025 9:00 amExit reflection. 17/11/2025 9:00 am
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning.See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Throughout the course, you will keep a reflective journal of your opportunity journey. Five times throughout the course, you will reflect on the progress that you make and the successes and challenges you faced while developing your opportunities based on the concepts, tools, and techniques from the course. Individual reflections do not need to (but can) be longer than 200 words / 2–3 minutes video and include entry and exit reflections:1. What new aspects did you add to your opportunity, and why?2. What aspect of your opportunity did you abandon, and why?3. What existing aspects of your opportunity will you double down on, and why?AI StatementArtificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.Read full task descriptionHide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
You have to submit your reflections individually via Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Please contact teaching staff directly if you are unable to submit your reflection.
Late submission
Apenaltyof 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Opportunity and Execution Pitch – Stakeholder Pitch
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning.See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: You will deliver a max 7 min investor pitch followed by a 20-30 min explanation summarising the logic and evidence underlying the proposed solution and how it was informed by the insights gained throughout the course. Submission will be a video recording of the pitch and explanation accompanied by an extended slide deck including the pitch deck and complementary explanatory slides outlining the logic and evidence. The investor pitch must demonstrate that the opportunity is viable, feasible, and desirable.AI StatementArtificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.Read full task descriptionHide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
One member per team has to submit the slides and video recording via Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

Late submission
Apenaltyof 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
